input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom input bar on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. Let me provide guidance based on what I know:

In .NET 9 and earlier, you could use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to make the window resize when the keyboard appears. However, in Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and in .NET 10 (which targets newer Android APIs), this behavior changed.


The shift in .NET 10 is that MAUI now defaults to edge-to-edge display handling following Android 15's requirements, which means the old resize approach no longer works as expected. The better solution is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or properly handle `WindowInsets` to manage the layout when the keyboard appears. Setting up keyboard inset listeners... Finishing the listener implementation...

Then I'm reconsidering this approach. In .NET MAUI with .NET 10, there's likely a more straightforward built-in solution since MAUI adopted edge-to-edge rendering for Android and deprecated `SoftInputMode` in favor of the `WindowInsets` API.
